paper_statements.corollary_4_5: If T(a)=P(a) for every action, optimal logits can be reproduced by an action-dependent linear recurrence initialized at an arbitrary finite vector.
paper_statements.theorem_5_9: For T(a)=P(a)+epsilon Q(a), under Assumption 5.8, the results of Theorem 5.7 hold for the time-varying ALF in Equation 65.

exact_corollary_acceptance: at least four genuinely different action-dependent permutation matrices
structural certificate of exactly one unit entry per row and column
explicit first-step correction for arbitrary initialization
general action-conditioned log-sum-exp and linear paths agree within 1e-12
a non-permutation action produces mismatch above 0.1
near_permutation_acceptance: T(a)=(1-epsilon)P(a)+epsilon U is column stochastic for every action
all emissions are positive and no columns are identical
all actions are exercised and supplied to ALF and LOF
valid sqrt(epsilon) ALF decreases with confidence separation
epsilon-squared and wrong-action controls are separated
overall_verdict_rule: The combined claim is VERIFIED only if both the exact corollary and universal asymptotic theorem are established. Finite Monte Carlo evidence cannot establish Theorem 5.9's inherited double limits.
